name: Whole Wheat French Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the yeast, sugar, lite salt, and water.
2. Gradually add the flours and mix well using your hands. The dough will be very sticky at first; add enough flour to transfer it to a lightly floured board.
3. Knead until the dough is no longer sticky (about 10 minutes), adding more flour as necessary. Place in a lightly oiled bowl.
4. Cover with a damp cloth and let rise in a warm place until doubled in volume (1 1/2-2 hours).
5. Punch down the dough. Transfer to a floured board and cut into four equal parts.
6. Roll and shape each part into a long loaf. Place loaves into a lightly oiled, special long-loaf pans (baguette pans).
7. Slash the top of each loaf diagonally in three or four places and brush with egg white and water mixture.
8. Let dough rise another hour or until doubled in volume.
9. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Bake the loaves until browned and hollow-sounding when thumped, about 25 minutes.
- If planning to freeze bread, underbake by baking for only 15 minutes. Wrap in foil when cool and freeze.
- When ready to serve, remove from freezer. Leave wrapped in foil. Place in a preheated oven at 350 degrees F (175 degrees C) for 10 minutes. Remove foil and continue to bake for 5 more minutes until crisp.
Makes 4 loaves, about 18 inches long.
